author: Torah Light Editorial Team

title.en: Shabbat: The Jewish Day of Rest — A Complete Guide
title.zh: 安息日(Shabbat):犹太人的休息日——完整指南
title.he: שבת: יום המנוחה היהודי — מדריך מלא

subtitle.en: Understanding and observing the weekly sanctuary in time
subtitle.zh: 理解并遵守每周的时间圣所
subtitle.he: הבנה ושמירה על מקדש שבועי בזמן

description.en: A comprehensive guide to Shabbat, covering its theological foundations, practical observance, prayers, blessings, prohibitions, meals, and how Chinese-Jewish families can adapt this ancient tradition. Includes full Hebrew texts with transliteration and translation.
description.zh: 关于安息日的全面指南，涵盖其神学基础、实际遵守、祷告、祝福、禁令、餐食，以及中国犹太家庭如何适应这一古老传统。包含完整的希伯来文本及音译和翻译。
description.he: מדריך מקיף לשבת, המכסה את היסודות התיאולוגיים שלה, שמירה מעשית, תפילות, ברכות, איסורים, סעודות, ואיך משפחות יהודיות-סיניות יכולות להתאים מסורת עתיקה זו. כולל טקסטים עבריים מלאים עם תעתיק ותרגום.

target_audience: anyone seeking to understand Shabbat observance
learning_objectives: Understand the theological meaning of Shabbat and its connection to creation
Learn how to prepare for Shabbat (cleaning, cooking, candle lighting times)
Master the Friday night rituals: candle lighting, Shalom Aleichem, Eishet Chayil, Kiddush, HaMotzi
Understand the 39 categories of prohibited creative work (melachot)
Learn what is permitted and encouraged on Shabbat
Perform Havdalah ceremony correctly with wine, spices, and fire
Understand how to start Shabbat observance gradually
Discover how Chinese families can adapt Shabbat traditions with familiar foods and values

key_concepts.definition_en: The Jewish day of rest, observed from Friday sunset to Saturday nightfall, commemorating God's rest on the seventh day of creation
Creative work prohibited on Shabbat; 39 categories derived from the construction of the Tabernacle
Sanctification blessing recited over wine at the beginning of Shabbat meals
key_concepts.significance: Declares the holiness of the day

key_concepts.definition_en: Separation ceremony marking the end of Shabbat, using wine, spices, and fire
key_concepts.significance: Distinguishes between sacred and mundane time

key_concepts.definition_en: Braided bread used on Shabbat; two loaves commemorate the double portion of manna
The 'additional soul' received on Shabbat, symbolizing heightened spiritual capacity

related_topics.relationship: Shabbat is the weekly foundation for understanding all Jewish holy days
Shabbat prayers form the basis of synagogue liturgy
Shabbat meal preparation requires advance kosher cooking techniques
Weekly Torah portion is read on Shabbat morning
Shabbat creates weekly family bonding and spiritual connection

prerequisites: None required for beginners - article explains from first principles
next_steps: Attend a Shabbat dinner in a Jewish home or synagogue
Practice lighting Shabbat candles for the first time
Learn to recite basic blessings in Hebrew
Study the weekly Torah portion (Parashat HaShavua)
Connect with local Jewish community for Shabbat hospitality
Explore deeper study of the 39 melachot

food_adaptations: Soy-braised chicken instead of roasted chicken
Steamed fish with ginger and scallions
Sweet red bean soup for dessert
Challah braided in Chinese knot patterns

value_connections.chinese_parallel: 团圆饭 (reunion dinner) - family gathering meals during festivals
孝 (filial piety) - Confucian value of honoring parents
Lunar calendar observances marking sacred time
Elaborate meal preparation as care and celebration

practical_tips: Use timer switches for lights (permitted when set before Shabbat)
Keep rice warm in thermal cooker prepared Friday
Join Chinese-speaking Jewish communities in Shanghai, Beijing, Hong Kong
Learn blessings in Chinese while gradually learning Hebrew
Invite family to observe without requiring full participation

hebrew_prayers_included.hebrew: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה׳ אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, אֲשֶׁר קִדְּשָׁנוּ בְּמִצְוֹתָיו וְצִוָּנוּ לְהַדְלִיק נֵר שֶׁל שַׁבָּת
hebrew_prayers_included.transliteration: Baruch atah Adonai, Eloheinu melech ha-olam, asher kid'shanu b'mitzvotav v'tzivanu l'hadlik ner shel Shabbat
hebrew_prayers_included.translation_en: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who has sanctified us with His commandments and commanded us to kindle the Shabbat light

practical_sections.title: Beginner's Start Guide
levels: Level 1: Light candles, special meal, unplug for a few hours
Level 2: Attend services, full tech Shabbat, study Torah portion
Level 3: Full traditional observance of all 39 melachot prohibitions

biblical_sources.theme: God's rest on the seventh day of creation
Fourth Commandment: Remember the Sabbath (creation focus)
Fourth Commandment: Observe the Sabbath (freedom from slavery focus)
Shabbat as eternal sign between God and Israel
Double portion of manna on Fridays

talmudic_references.topic: Welcoming Shabbat as a queen or bride
The additional soul (neshamah yeteirah) on Shabbat

multimedia_suggestions.style: Chinese ink wash painting (shan shui 山水)
multimedia_suggestions.description: Shabbat table at twilight with candlesticks, challah, kiddush cup, traditional pavilion, plum blossoms, misty mountains

multimedia_suggestions.suggested_format: grouped by theme with modern examples

accessibility_features: Cultural bridge explanations for Chinese readers
Beginner to advanced progressive learning levels
